university.name: Greenfield University
university.description: A mid-sized off-grid university campus with 8 load points, powered by a 500 kW solar PV array, a 150 kW diesel generator, and a 1,200 kWh battery bank.

controller_rules.name: Solar-first dispatch
controller_rules.description: Solar PV is always used first to serve loads and charge the battery.

controller_rules.name: Battery pre-charge bias
controller_rules.description: 10% of solar output is diverted to charge the battery BEFORE serving any load, reducing power available to loads during daylight hours.

controller_rules.name: [Late diesel trigger
controller_rules.description: Diesel only starts when SOC ≤ 125 kWh AND solar < 10 kW — far too late, causing prolonged shedding before diesel intervenes.

controller_rules.name: High load-shedding threshold
controller_rules.description: A load point is dropped if available power < 80% of its demand, causing over-aggressive shedding even with a modest shortfall.

controller_rules.name: Night priority  ( — ACTIVE)
controller_rules.description: At night (18:00–06:00), LP8 (Street Lighting) is elevated to priority-2: LP1 > LP8 > LP4 > LP2 > LP3 > LP5 > LP6 > LP7. During the day: LP1 > LP2 > LP3 > LP4 > LP5 > LP6 > LP7 > LP8.

controller_rules.name: Diesel always dispatched at full capacity
controller_rules.description: When diesel starts, it always runs at full 150 kW regardless of actual shortfall, wasting fuel.

controller_rules.name: Battery discharge
controller_rules.description: Battery discharges to cover deficit between solar (after pre-charge) and load demand, up to 200 kW, down to 120 kWh SOC floor.

files.source_availability.csv: 8,760 rows × 19 cols. Solar, diesel, battery, fuel cost.
files.university_metadata.json: Full spec, load points, sources, controller rules, reliability.
